properties.remark: CHP REPORTED A MUDSLIDE ALONG HIGHWAY 138 NEAR CAJON JUNCTION. BOTH DIRECTIONS OF THE HIGHWAY CLOSED.
BLOWING SAND PRODUCING NEAR-ZERO VISIBILITY ALONG HIGHWAY 247 NEAR LUCERNE VALLEY.
NUMEROUS TREES DAMAGED FROM WIND IN SYLVAN PARK IN BANNING, CALIFORNIA DURING A THUNDERSTORM. TIME OF DAMAGE IS APPROXIMATE.
MUDFLOW ACROSS HIGHWAY 38 NEAR VALLEY OF THE FALLS DRIVE. FLOW ABOUT 2-3 FEET DEEP AND 30-40 FEET WIDE ROAD CLOSED DUE TO FIRE BUT NOW IMPASSABLE.
MUDFLOW 100 FEET WIDE AND 10 FEET DEEP REPORTED ON HIGHWAY 38.
WINDS DOWNED A POWER POLE/LINE ON TO A HOUSE, IGNITING IT ON FIRE. WIND GUSTS IN INDIO 31 MPH.
HIGHWAY 18 CLOSED BETWEEN BIG BEAR DAM AND SNOW VALLEY DUE TO NUMEROUS ROCK SLIDES IN THE ARCTIC CIRCLE.
1.5 FT DEEP FLOOD WATER ON MECHLEIS RD. FLASH FLOODING HAS ALSO BEEN REPORTED ON HWY 18. 0.87 RAIN ACCUM SO FAR IN THE PAST HOUR. PEA SIZE HAIL AND OCNL LTGICCG HAS BEEN OBSERVED.

KENT MILLER CALLED AND REPORTED A FLASH FLOOD ON FISH CREEK WASH NEAR SPLIT CREEK ROAD. THE DIRT ROAD HAD APPROXIMATELY A FOOT OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS IT. HE SENT A VIDEO. SPOTTER STATED THERE WAS NO HAIL AND VERY LITTLE LIGHTNING IN THAT AREA.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 63 MPH WAS RECORDED AT PALM SPRINGS AIRPORT.
CHP REPORTS THAT THE ROADWAY IS FLOODED AND IMPASSABLE AT HACIENDA AVE. AND LONG CANYON RD.
FLOODING ON HIGHWAY 247 AT LUCERNE ROAD REPORTED BY CHP AND CALTRANS.
CHP REPORTS ROCKS AND DEBRIS BLOCKING ROADWAY AT DILLON RD AND BUBBLING WELLS RD.
MUDSLIDE 6 FEET DEEP EXTENDING 20 FEET ACROSS HIGHWAY 18 REPORTED BY CHP. 12 VEHICLES STRANDED.
CALTRANS AND CHP REPORT VALLEY OF THE FALLS CLOSED DUE TO DEBRIS OR MUD FLOWS FROM HEAVY RAIN AND CONCERNS FOR MORE MOVEMENT. THIS IS JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 38.
FLASH FLOODING ON HIGHWAY 18 MILE MARKER 65 NEAR MITSUBISHI CEMENT CORPORATION PLANT. NUMEROUS VEHICLES STRANDED DUE TO SIGNIFICANT FLOW OF WATER AND DEBRIS. HIGHWAY 18 CLOSED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS.
STATE ROUTE 18 WILL BE CLOSED BETWEEN BIG BEAR AND LUCERNE VALLEY UNTIL FUTHER NOTICE DUE TO FLOODING.
REPORT OF QUARTER-SIZED HAIL SOUTH OF ANGELUS OAKS ON HIGHWAY 38.
TWITTER VIDEO REPORT OF HEAVY MUD AND DEBRIS FLOW ON VALLEY OF THE FALLS DRIVE IN FOREST FALLS.
PUBLIC AND FIRE RADIO REPORTS THAT VALLEY OF THE FALLS DR IS CLOSED DUE TO A DEBRIS FLOW.
CHP REPORTED A MUD/DIRT/ROCK SLIDE ON HWY 38 BETWEEN ONYX PEAK AND SUGARLOAF.
CHP REPORTS CAMP ROCK RD IS FLOODED OUT AND ROCKS AND DEBRIS ACROSS THE ROAD.
CHP REPORTS WATER AND MUD FLOODED ROADWAY ON HWY 247 AND LOCUS AVE.
CHP REPORTS BROOKSIDE AVE AND HIGHLAND SPRINGS AVE FLOODED OUT ON BOTH SIDES OF THE ROAD.
CHP REPORTS A VEHICLE WENT OFF THE ROAD AND INTO A POTHOLE, AND IS NOW BEING FLOODED WITH WATER.
MULTIPLE PHOTOS OF LANDSPOUT TORNADO ASSOCIATED WITH SEVERE THUNDERSTORM OVER ANZA VALLEY.
CALTRANS REPORTED DOZENS OF DEBRIS FLOWS NEAR THE CRANSTON FIRE BURN SCAR, SOME OF THEM FLOWING ONTO HWY 74.
PUBLIC REPORTED A DEBRIS FLOW AT THE SHEEP CREEK WASH IN WRIGHTWOOD ON LONE PINE CANYON RD. LONE PINE CANYON RD. IS SHUT DOWN DUE TO THE DEBRIS FLOW.
CHP REPORTS A ROCK/DIRT/MUD SLIDE ALONG HWY 38 BETWEEN ONYX PEAK AND SUGARLOAF. INDICATED THE ROAD WAS COVERED WITH ROCKS.
CHP REPORTS A SLIDE WITH MUD/ROCK ACROSS THE ROADWAY ON HIGHWAY 74 IN PINYON PINES NEAR THE FIRE STATION.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM 10 SW INDIAN WELLS. CHP REPORTS BOULDERS ACROSS THE ROADWAY ON HIGHWAY 74 IN PINYON PINES NEAR THE FIRE STATION.
SHERIFF REPORTS MAJOR DEBRIS FLOW IN THE ROAD ON HWY 74 ON THE CRANSTON FIRE BURN SCAR.

INDIVIDUALS IN VEHICLES UNABLE TO CROSS VIVIAN AND MOMYER CREEKS DUE TO FAST-MOVING WATER IN THE CHANNEL. THE WATER IS FROM EARLIER THUNDERSTORM ACTIVITY UPSTREAM. EMERGENCY PERSONNEL HAVE BEEN DISPATCHED TO THE LOCATION TO ASSIST MOTORISTS. THESE ARE NOT SWIFT-WATER RESCUES.
MUD AND ROCKS OVER HIGHWAY 78 NEAR BUTTES PASS ROAD.
MEDIA REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN INDIO SHORTLY AFTER 11 PM.
CHP REPORTS HEAVY RAIN IN MECCA AREA. ROADS BEGINNING TO FLOOD.
I-10 FLOODING. WATER 1.5 FEET DEEP AND 12 FEET WIDE OVER THE INTERSTATE.
RAIN AND ROCKS ALL OVER EASTBOUND I-10. UNABLE TO DRIVE ON.
ROAD FLOODED AT LINCOLN ST AND 63RD AVE IN MECCA.
ROAD FLOODED AT VARNER DRIVE AND DATE PALM DRIVE. CHP REQUESTED ROAD CLOSURE.
ROAD COMPLETELY FLOODED AT NORTH INDIAN CANYON DR AND HIGHWAY 62. SUV STALLED IN LANE 1.
ROAD FLOODED AT MONTEREY AVE AND RAMON ROAD IN THOUSAND PALMS. VEHICLES UNABLE TO GET THROUGH.
DILLON ROAD IN SKY VALLEY FLOODED AT EAST DECEPTION ROAD.
MEDIA REPORTS HIGHWAY 60 IS FLOODED NEAR ETIWANDA AVE, WITH ACCIDENTS IN THE AREA.
CALFIRE VIDEO OF LARGE GREY WATER FLOW IN RICE CANYON IN TEMESCAL VALLEY.
SPOTTER VIDEO OF SIGNIFICANT GREY WATER FLOW IN HORSETHIEF CANYON WITHIN BANKS.
CALFIRE REPORTS WATER AND MUD ON GLEN IVY ROAD NEAR THE HOLY FIRE BURN SCAR.
COLDWATER CREEK REPORTED TO BE FLOWING OVER GLEN IVY ROAD PER CALFIRE. DEBRIS AND WATER MIXTURE.
CALTRANS REPORTS AN EMERGENCY CLOSURE AT SR-38 AT VALLEY OF THE FALLS DRIVE DUE TO ROCKS AND MUD IN THE ROAD. ALL LANES ARE BLOCKED FOR AN UNKNOWN DURATION.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM 3 WNW FOREST FALLS. CALTRANS REPORTS AN EMERGENCY CLOSURE AT SR-38 AT VALLEY OF THE FALLS DRIVE THROUGH ANGELUS OAKS DUE TO ROCKS AND MUD IN THE ROAD. ALL LANES ARE BLOCKED FOR AN UNKNOWN DURATION.
RIVCO EM REPORTS WATER AND ROCKS IN THE ROAD NEAR TEMESCAL CANYON. APPEARS THAT IT IS MOSTLY WATER WITH SEVERAL ROCKS IN THE ROAD.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM 9 ENE MISSION VIEJO. MEDIA REPORTS MAJOR DEBRIS FLOW AT TRABUCO CREEK. THE DEBRIS FLOW LOOKED COMPRISED OF VERY LITTLE WATER AND MOSTLY DEBRIS/MUD.
MEDIA REPORTS MAJOR DEBRIS FLOW AT TRABUCO CREEK. THE DEBRIS FLOW LOOKED COMPRISED OF VERY LITTLE WATER AND MOSTLY DEBRIS/MUD.
SPOTTER REPORTS MINOR COASTAL FLOODING IN SUNSET BEACH ALONG THE PACIFIC COAST HIGHWAY AND CROSS STREETS INCLUDING BROADWAY AND ANDERSON.
CHP REPORTED HWY 18 BETWEEN JOHNSON AND WILSON RANCH FLOODED IN MULTIPLE PLACES.
PUBLIC REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ALONG HWY 55 AT PAULARINO AVE. REPORTED APPROXIMATELY ONE FOOT OF WATER ALONG THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE ROAD.
SPOTTER REPORTED HEAVY RAIN AND FLASH FLOODING ALONG ORANGETHORPE BETWEEN EUCLID AND STATE COLLEGE BLVD. REPORTED THAT ROADS ARE FLOODING, ESPECIALLY AT INTERSECTIONS.
SPOTTER REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ALONG ORANGETHORPE, WEST OF RAYMOND STREET. REPORTED 8-12 INCHES OF WATER ON THE CURB.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM 4 SSW COSTA MESA. TWO SCHOOLS EVACUATED DUE TO FLOODING.
FROM FACEBOOK.
LAGUNA BEACH POLICE REPORTED THAT COAST HWY WAS CLOSED IN SOUTH LAGUNA BEACH BETWEEN 3RD AND 9TH AVE DUE TO FLOODING.
CHP REPORTS GRAND AVE COMPLETELY WASHED OUT DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.

5 BUSINESSES REPORTED FLOOD DAMAGE FROM HEAVY RAINFALL INCLUDING ORANGE THEORY GYM WHICH WAS FORCED TO CLOSE FOR TODAY. UNKNOWN IF THERE WERE DRAINAGE BLOCKAGES. THUNDERSTORMS WITH VERY HEAVY RAIN MOVED OVER THIS REGION. SIMILAR FLOODING OCCURRED UPSTREAM IN OCEANSIDE ON PACIFIC STREET.

NWS STORM SURVEY ON DECEMBER 7TH FOUND SEVERAL NEIGHBORHOODS WITH EXTENSIVE MUD, ROCK AND WATER FLOODING. DEBRIS LINE WAS UP TO 3 FEET ON MAILBOXES AND EYEWITNESSES REPORTED THIS DEPTH. THERE WERE A COUPLE HOMES WITH MUD DAMAGE IN LEACH CANYON ROAD. ENTIRE STREETS HAD MUD 6 TO 18 INCHES DEEP WITH DOZENS OF EQUIPMENT ACTIVELY REMOVING MUD AND ROCKS ON STREETS AT THE TIME OF SURVEY. THIS IS RELATED TO THE HOLY BURN SCAR. DEBRIS RAN DOWN GRAND AVENUE CAUSING CLOSURES AND UP TO THE LAKE ELSINORE SHORELINE. CONCRETE CHANNELS FOR NORMAL WATER FLOW WERE OVERWHELMED AND OVERTOPPED WITH WATER AND DEBRIS COVERING YARDS AND PRIVATE DRIVEWAYS.
VICTOR VALLEY NEWS GROUP REPORTED THAT DEL GADO ROAD BETWEEN ANACAPA AND PALMDALE ROAD IS CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. THREE VEHICLES WERE STRANDED, BUT NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.
ROADWAY FLOODING ON NATIONAL TRAILS HWY AT MILL STREET.
CHP REPORTED THAT A SIGNIFICANT DEBRIS FLOW WAS NEAR GLEN EDEN MOBILE HOME PARK OFF OF DE PALMA ROAD.
LAKE ELSINORE POLICE REPORTED THAT WATER AND MUD BREACHED A K-RAIL IN A NEIGHBORHOOD.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS LONG CANYON ROAD AT SR 74 COMPLETELY WASHED OUT.
FLASH FLOODING AND DEBRIS FLOW. 20 RESIDENTS REQUIRING RESCUE ON COBBLESTONE LN IN LAKE ELSINORE.

GLEN EDEN RECEIVED A HEAVY AMOUNT OF DAMAGE. LOST A PEDESTRIAN BRIDGE. LOTS OF MUD AND DEBRIS AND LARGE BOULDERS. THERE WAS A LARGE WOOD BRIDGE DISPLACED AND A STRANDED VEHICLE IN THE MIDDLE OF DEBRIS (MOTORIST WENT AROUND BARRICADES) ON CORONA LAKES SIDE EAST OF I-15.

LARGE TREE STUMPS FROM HOLY FIRE SCAR IN HORSETHIEF CANYON, LARGE BOULDERS AND MUD DEBRIS 6 FEET DEEP COVERED TEMESCAL CANYON ROAD. A OLD FOOT BRIDGE WAS REMOVED AND SPLIT IN 2 PIECES ON EITHER SIDE OF THE ROAD. AT THE TIME OF THE SURVEY AT 1 PM DECEMBER 7TH THE ROAD WAS CLOSED AND HEAVY EQUIPMENT WAS DOING DEBRIS REMOVAL. THIS DEBRIS FIELD EXTENDED TOWARDS LAKE CORONA AND UNDER INTERSTATE 15.
BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER FLOODED COAST HIGHWAY NEAR DOWNTOWN OCEANSIDE. WATER WAS GOING UP TO THE CURB NEAR BUSINESSES. ONE RESCUE REPORTED.
SPOTTER REPORTS MODERATE STREET FLOODING AT THE INTERSECTION OF MISSION AVE AND EL CAMINO REAL IN OCEANSIDE.
OTAY LAKES RD. WASHED OUT. THE ROAD WAS CLOSED BETWEEN THOUSAND TRAILS CAMPGROUND AND HWY 94.
REPORT OF FLOWING WATER THAT HAS SPILLED OVER FROM THE ROAD ONTO THE SIDEWALKS.
TIME ESTIMATED. FEDERAL BLVD WAS CLOSED WITH LARGE AMOUNT OF DEBRIS FROM FLOWING WATER ON STREET BETWEEN 60TH STREET AND COLLEGE AVE.

CORONADO POLICE REPORTED WIDESPREAD FLOODING WHERE 7-8 CARS WERE FULLY SUBMERGED AT 4TH STREET AND ALAMEDA BOULEVARD, AND PARTS OF SILVER STRAND BOULEVARD FLOODED NEAR CORONADO CAYS. BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED.

NATIONAL CITY 30 PEOPLE WERE FORCED FROM FLOODED UNITS IN AN APARTMENT COMPLEX ON DELTA STREET NEAR 43RD STREET. DOZENS OF DRIVERS WERE FORCED TO ABANDON THEIR FLOODED VEHICLES ON 16TH STREET NEAR NATIONAL CITY BOULEVARD AS WATER RESCUE TEAMS HELPED THEM TO SAFETY.ALSO IN NATIONAL CITY, CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL CLOSED THE RIGHT TWO LANES OF STATE ROUTE 54 AND THE NORTHBOUND CONNECTOR RAMP TO INTERSTATE 805 SO CREWS COULD REPAIR DRAINS TO REDUCE FLOODING. AMERICAN RED CROSS OPENED A SHELTER IN NATIONAL CITY FOR PEOPLE AFFECTED BY THE FLOODS AND WHO DO NOT HAVE A PLACE TO STAY. THE SHELTER WILL BE AT THE PARADISE VALLEY SEVENTH DAY ADVENTIST CHURCH LOCATED AT 2701 EAST 8TH STREET.
BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED POLICE CLOSED INTERSECTION OF LAUREL STREET AND PACIFIC HIGHWAY DUE TO FLOODING AND WATER RESCUES. THUNDERSTORMS WITH VERY HEAVY RAIN TRACKED OVER THIS AREA FROM 5 TO 8 PM.

SAN DIEGO LIFEGUARD SWIFTWATER REPORTED 7-8 VEHICLES FLOATING IN DEEP WATER AND 2 VEHICLES WERE OCCUPIED REQUIRING SWIFT WATER RESCUES. 19TH AND COMMERCIAL AVE. RADAR ESTIMATED 2 INCHES OF RAIN IN 2 HOURS WITH THUNDERSTORMS.

SAN DIEGO METROPOLITAN TRANSIT SYSTEM SAID FLOODED TRACKS WERE CAUSING DELAYS AND DETOURS. AT 8 P.M. DECEMBER 6TH, THE FASHION VALLEY AND PLAZA BONITA STATIONS WERE NOT IN SERVICE. AS OF 6:40 P.M., BUS BRIDGES WERE IN PLACE FOR THE ORANGE LINE FROM 47TH STREET TO PARK BOULEVARD AND MARKET STREET, THE UCSD BLUE LINE FROM 8TH STREET TO PARK BOULEVARD AND THE SYCUAN GREEN LINE FROM COUNTY CENTER/LITTLE ITALY TO 12TH STREET AND IMPERIAL AVENUE.

NWS SURVEY SHOWED THE 12 FOOT REPORTED STAGE ON THE SAN DIEGO RIVER WITH MUD AND DEBRIS LINE. 9 FOOT STAGE AT THE TIME OF THE 10 AM PST DECEMBER 7TH SURVEY WITH A SMALL SECONDARY RISE OCCURRING. ABANDON VEHICLE FOUND THAT HAD BEEN IN 3-4 FEET OF WATER AND MUD. SAN DIEGO RIVER AT FASHION VALLEY WENT FROM 4.5 FEET AT 5 PM TO 12.08 FEET AT 1030 PM.

IN THE EAST VILLAGE, A HOMELESS SHELTER WAS EVACUATED DUE TO FLOODING. MEDIA VIDEO SHOWED WATER THAT WAS WAIST DEEP. NEARLY 300 EVACUEES WERE BUSSED TO SDCCU STADIUM, WHERE AMERICAN RED CROSS ALSO PROVIDED SUPPLIES.
QUARRY ROAD IN SPRING VALLEY WAS CLOSED AROUND 10:30 AM DUE TO WATER ON THE ROAD.
